name: Ed Barlow
tagline: Software engineer, database architect, and published author. Building thoughtful systems for complex problems.
home_html:
Senior Principal Engineer with 30 years building enterprise platforms at hedge funds, health systems, and startups. Now applying that foundation to AI-assisted software development.

I help small and mid-sized businesses adopt AI development practices — not just tools, but the workflow, governance, and project structure that makes AI actually deliver.

The Framework

Most teams get AI wrong. They add a coding assistant but keep the same chaotic workflow. The result: faster generation of the same mess. My approach is different.

I bring a structured framework — GAME (Generic Agent Management Environment) — that gives every project a standard shape: defined stack, governed operations, observable processes, and AI prompts tuned to each development stage.

  • Structured Workflow — features move through a defined lifecycle from Idea to Done, with Agent Assist at each gate
  • Governed Operations — every project's start, stop, test, and deploy scripts are registered automatically and run from a single dashboard
  • Observable by Default — heartbeats, process logs, and usage analytics built in from day one
  • Markdown as Configuration — your stack rules, project metadata, and Claude instructions live in plain text files you own and version

View the system architecture →

How It Works

A new project gets a standard shape on day one: a CLAUDE.md that tells the AI assistant exactly how to work in this codebase, a STACK.yaml that declares technology rules, and bin/ scripts with headers that self-register as UI buttons. The AI knows the conventions. You don't repeat yourself.

See the feature development lifecycle →

See how bin/ scripts integrate with the platform →

Engagements

I work with teams who are serious about building well, not just building fast. Typical engagements: platform setup and methodology, AI-assisted sprint acceleration, architecture review, and hands-on development.

Reach out via the Contact link above.
